title: Air China Cargo ramps up Glasgow Prestwick operation

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Established in 1983, Air Cargo News magazine provides news, insights and features, as well as data and analysis for the international airfreight and logistics industry through its website, e-newsletters and print magazine. Content includes developments across airlines, freight forwarders, airports, ground handlers, GSSAs and supply chains worldwide. Air China Cargo has expanded its operation between Chengdu and Glasgow Prestwick Airport to daily on the back of rising demand. The service, which operates between Prestwick and Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport, had been operating four times per week until yesterday, when the frequency was increased.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: The service largely carries e-commerce from China, but in the opposite direction, takes Scottish exports such as salmon, seafood and whisky. The Scottish airport said that Air China Cargo’s new daily service brings the total number of scheduled cargo flights to 15 direct to China, with the trade routes expected to facilitate over £250m of cross-border trade in 2026. The airline launched services to the airport in June last year. “The move to daily flights to Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport reflects the strength of demand for direct cargo connectivity between the UK and China,” said Ian Forgie, chief executive, Glasgow Prestwick Airport.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: “Prestwick is increasingly recognised by international carriers as a strategic cargo gateway linking the UK with key Chinese logistics hubs, enabling Scottish exporters to reach major regional markets quickly and reliably.”

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Air China Cargo ramps up Glasgow Prestwick operation
